title: Ops lead reconciles failed order exports every morning
text: The ops lead spends 45 minutes every morning exporting failed orders, checking spreadsheet rows, and deciding which support replies are safe to send.

title: Failed exports require manual spreadsheet checklist
text: When exports fail, support waits for ops to copy rows into a spreadsheet checklist. Missed rows become delayed customer updates.

title: Finance copies invoice handoffs between three tools
text: The finance lead spends two hours each Friday copying invoice data between tools because edge cases break the handoff and create billing mistakes.

title: Support supervisors rebuild escalation summaries manually
text: Supervisors rebuild escalation summaries from chat logs, ticket comments, and account notes before weekly customer-risk meetings.

title: Implementation manager tracks onboarding blockers in side spreadsheet
text: The implementation manager keeps a side spreadsheet of onboarding blockers because the project tool does not show which customer tasks are actually stuck.

title: Vendor compliance evidence is collected by email thread
text: A compliance coordinator asks vendors for the same policy screenshots every quarter and tracks missing evidence in email threads.

title: Security reviews stall while teams chase vendor answers
text: Security review owners wait days for vendor answers, then paste the responses into a risk sheet for the approver.

title: Quarterly vendor evidence repeats from scratch
text: The buyer says every quarterly review feels like starting over because prior vendor evidence is hard to trust or reuse.

title: Controller does not trust cash reports
text: The owner asks the controller to explain why cash reports differ from bank balances before any weekly decisions are made.

title: Owner trusts reports but hates preparation time
text: The owner says the reports are accurate enough, but preparing them takes too long before Monday meetings.

title: Payroll exception approvals happen in Slack
text: Payroll exceptions are posted in Slack, then copied into the payroll system after managers approve them.

title: Manager approvals disappear before audit
text: A people ops manager screenshots approval threads because the audit trail across Slack and payroll is unreliable.

title: Clinic admin phones patients after broken intake forms
text: The clinic admin calls patients when intake forms are incomplete because the EHR does not show which field blocked scheduling.

title: Incomplete forms create same-day rescheduling scramble
text: Front desk staff discover missing intake details on appointment day and scramble to reschedule or call patients.

title: Maybe we need something for renewals
text: Renewals are weird lately. Somebody should probably look at it before end of quarter.

title: CSM builds renewal risk notes from scattered usage clues
text: The CSM copies usage drops, support sentiment, and contract notes into a renewal-risk doc before account reviews.

title: Customer success cannot explain churn risk quickly
text: CS managers ask analysts for usage exports before they can explain why a renewal is at risk.

title: Dashboard request with no buyer
text: Need a dashboard for everything because leadership keeps asking questions.

title: Warehouse lead audits exception picks by hand
text: The warehouse lead prints exception picks each shift and marks which ones were caused by inventory mismatch versus picker error.

title: Inventory mismatch root cause is rebuilt from notes
text: Ops analysts rebuild inventory mismatch root cause from picker notes, WMS exports, and photos after shipment misses.

title: Ambiguous AI note request
text: They asked if AI could help with notes, but it was unclear whether the problem is speed, accuracy, compliance, or follow-up ownership.

title: Agency owner checks ad spend anomalies before client calls
text: The agency owner manually compares platform spend, CRM leads, and yesterday's screenshots before explaining anomalies to clients.
